patterns_recognized: F1_xera_gap* without F3 >= 1000 produces redundant corroboration: validated 4+ consecutive suppressions through 7/17 (LAD@NYY F1*=1.61 no F3, CHW@TOR F1*=1.87 no F3, both lost). Starred SP sample means F1 and SP xERA draw from the same thin Statcast pool — not independent anchors.

GOLD CONFIRM at the 65% home-favorite boundary requires an F-signal beyond BvP OWNERs: 7/17 BAL@HOU (pick_prob=65.3%, two OWNERs Altuve+Alvarez vs Kremer, no F-signal) was a P&L loss 3-2. BvP career splits share informational content with p_model and cannot serve as sole independent corroboration.

HARD CAP 7 has one confirmed false-negative in the verified postgame archive: KC@BAL 7/10 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.018 (well below the 0.04 meaningful threshold) and BAL won 5-3 anyway. Today's MIA@MIL sits at +0.0185, essentially the same range. Note: an earlier version of this file cited a second '7/12 KC@BAL +0.0189, BAL won 8-2' data point — no postgame/2026-07-12.json exists in the archive to verify that claim, so it is being dropped as unconfirmed rather than repeated. One verified break (not two) is not enough evidence to override CAP 7 today.

Stage 2 high-delta direction reliable unless |pqi_diff| > 20 and PQI opposes: first documented failure 7/17 STL@ARI (f5_full_delta=0.186, PQI delta=-25.8 opposing Stage 2, STL won 5-4). Otherwise the streak holds — do not cite Stage 2 direction as soft confirmation when large PQI strongly opposes.

Live statsapi check (run at final-pitch refresh) confirms LAD @ NYY has been POSTPONED for inclement weather and rescheduled to 2026-07-19 — no game will be played today regardless of the model's GOLD tag on NYY. Also confirmed Owen Murphy (ATL) and Patrick Sandoval (BOS) are indeed today's starters despite thin-Statcast hard-veto flags — no late scratch on either thin-SP-data game.

PIT @ CLE.reasoning: SKIP on three grounds: edge +2.46pp is below the 4pp valid floor; f5_full_delta=0.2691 (Stage 1 gives CLE 75.65% F5 while Stage 2 flips to PIT 51.3%); HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.513 with no F-signal present. SP edge 0.81 (Jared Jones) and PQI delta=-8.1 are genuinely positive but cannot rescue CAP 10 without an F-signal anchor. Stage 2 direction toward PIT is consistent with the high-delta archive pattern but no stake qualifies without four dimensions.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 four-dimension rescue requires F-signal

MIN @ CHC.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals: Taj Bradley (MIN) holds a 1.08 xERA advantage over Matthew Boyd (CHC), PQI delta=-10.8 opposes CHC, and edge is -8.89pp (HARD CAP 1 fires). pick_prob=0.502 is the slate's closest coin-flip. D-grade dual-opposing (SP against + PQI against) has been the July archive's most reliable SKIP indicator — validated repeatedly through 7/17. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-opposing SP+PQI = reliable SKIP (7/11 through 7/17 archive)

CHW @ TOR.reasoning: Two positive dimensions (PQI delta=+8.5, Stage agree delta=0.03) but no F-signal and no SP-xERA dimension in grade_reasons. HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.530. Without an F-signal anchor, the two-dimension stack does not meet the CAP 10 four-dimension rescue threshold. Edge 4.73pp is at the valid-window floor but post-cap score is already 0. This is a different starter pairing (Bieber/Martin) than the 7/17 CHW@TOR loss (which had a starred F1 signal); no direct precedent, just standard bottom-bucket suppression applies. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 rescue requires F+SP+PQI+Stage simultaneously — two dimensions insufficient

CIN @ COL.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals: Rhett Lowder (CIN) holds a 1.59 xERA advantage over Tomoyuki Sugano (COL), PQI delta=-9.8 opposes COL, edge +1.31pp is below the 4pp floor. Stage 1/2 agree (delta=0.00) is the sole positive dimension. Note: 7/17 flagged CIN@COL as the strongest DOWNGRADE false-negative (CIN won 7-2) — but today's pick is COL, the opposite side of that lesson, with the same SP-against configuration that drove CIN's win then. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: 7/17 CIN@COL false-negative: CIN won, not COL — pick direction differs today

NYM @ PHI.reasoning: D-grade with structural SKIP on multiple gates: fair_prob=0.373 is below the 0.42 viability floor; Jesus Luzardo (PHI) holds a 1.26 xERA advantage over Sean Manaea (NYM); PQI delta=+25.4 opposes NYM; edge 16.46pp exceeds the 15pp ceiling. Market pricing NYM at 37.3% while model assigns 53.7% is a material calibration gap consistent with the hallucination-zone pattern (7/17 TEX@ATL, 7/17 WSH@OAK) — those cases split (one loss, one win despite opposition), so no directional lean is drawn either way. Cannot verify live injury context. CONFIRM SKIP.

BAL @ HOU.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals approaching the home-favorite scrutiny boundary: Trevor Rogers (BAL) holds a 0.93 xERA advantage over Spencer Arrighetti (HOU), PQI delta=-12.6 opposes HOU. pick_prob=0.644 is just below the >65% over-confidence zone. Yesterday's BAL@HOU (7/17, different starters) was the slate's sole staked GOLD CONFIRM and lost 3-2 on BvP-driven conviction without an F-signal. Today's version is already correctly graded down to SKIP by dual opposition; no elevation warranted. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: 7/17 BAL@HOU GOLD loss (BvP OWNERs insufficient without F-signal)

SD @ KC.reasoning: GOLD label overstates conviction: F3_swing_take_gap=613.5 is well below the 1000 meaningful threshold — per the 7/11 archive, sub-1000 F3 does not anchor a GOLD pick on its own. Edge +0.16pp is trivially positive and outside the [4,15]pp valid window. HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.519. F5_bullpen_gap=+1.44 is a soft positive; hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.039 shows SD's bullpen marginally better but sub-0.04 (not meaningful). Without a valid F3 anchor or in-range edge, GOLD is misleading. DOWNGRADE to SKIP.
lessons_applied: F3 < 1000 is not a meaningful anchor (7/11 PHI@DET: F3=328.8 correctly suppressed)
Edge outside [4,15]pp valid window blocks stake

STL @ ARI.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals and negative edge: Dustin May (STL) holds a 0.68 xERA advantage over Brandon Pfaadt (ARI), PQI delta=-6.5 opposes ARI, edge -0.34pp. The pre-cap score was already -2 (D-grade) from the SP+PQI opposition alone, so HARD CAP 1 isn't structurally required here (it exists to catch GOLD-or-higher picks); the negative edge simply reinforces an already-correct SKIP. Yesterday's STL@ARI (7/17) noted Stage 2 high-delta direction failed when |pqi_diff| > 20 opposing — today's PQI opposition is moderate (6.5), well below that threshold, so that exception does not apply here. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: Negative edge reinforces D-grade (no GOLD to cap)
7/17 STL@ARI PQI-vs-Stage-2 lesson (|pqi|>20 threshold not met today)

TB @ BOS.reasoning: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO fires because Patrick Sandoval has only 65 Statcast pitches (needs 100+) — SP xERA is unreliable. Edge -1.21pp is negative (HARD CAP 1). HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.519. PQI delta=-8.5 also opposes BOS. Live statsapi confirms Sandoval is indeed today's starter (no late scratch; game in progress), and the 7/17 TB@BOS(G2) precedent validated this exact HARD_VETO gate. CONFIRM SKIP.

TEX @ ATL.reasoning: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO fires because Owen Murphy (ATL home SP) has only 67 Statcast pitches — SP data unreliable for the picked team's own starter. Edge -1.03pp triggers HARD CAP 1 (pre-cap score 3->1). HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.508 (score 1->0). Pre-cap stack was otherwise positive (PQI +5.2, Stage agree, F-signal), but Murphy's data gate plus negative edge block any rescue. Live statsapi confirms Murphy is indeed starting today (game now Final, ATL lost 7-6), no late scratch. CONFIRM SKIP.

MIA @ MIL.reasoning: Strong pre-cap stack (score=5: F3=3225.7, F5=+0.86, SP edge 0.41 agrees, PQI +7.2 confirms, Stage agree) — the exact 4-dimension profile that has qualified for a CAP-10 rescue exception elsewhere in the archive (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA, both wins). But HARD CAP 7 fires first on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0185, nearly identical to the +0.018 gap that produced CAP 7's one verified backtest break (7/10 KC@BAL, BAL won 5-3). One confirmed break out of the cap's larger sample is not enough evidence to override a validated cap. DOWNGRADE to SKIP; the marginal-gap coincidence is worth continued tracking, not action today.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 7 + HARD CAP 10 stacking — both caps authoritative
CAP 10 4-dimension rescue exception does not extend past an independent CAP 7 trigger
CAP 7 sub-0.04 gap false-negative tracking (1 verified break, 7/10 KC@BAL)

SF @ SEA.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals: Bryan Woo (SEA) holds a 0.45 xERA advantage over Logan Webb (SF), PQI delta=+7.1 opposes SF. Edge 8.09pp is within the valid window but D-grade correctly overrides tier eligibility. Yesterday's SF@SEA (different starters, SF won 7-0 against an identical all-signals-opposing profile) was explicitly flagged in the postgame as a pure-variance outlier not to be extrapolated. CONFIRM SKIP; no directional lean drawn from that result.
lessons_applied: 7/17 SF@SEA variance-win warning — do not generalize

LAD @ NYY.reasoning: GOLD label on a D-grade pick (pre-cap score=-1): only F5_bullpen_gap=+0.98 supports NYY; SP edge 0.51 xERA AGAINST NYY (Emmet Sheehan LAD over Ryan Weathers NYY) and PQI delta=-11.0 opposing NYY pull the score negative. F5-only without F1 or F3 is an insufficient anchor per the 7/17 LAD@NYY DOWNGRADE precedent (same matchup, correctly lost 2-1). DOWNGRADE to SKIP. IMPORTANT: live statsapi confirms this game has been POSTPONED (inclement weather) and rescheduled to 2026-07-19 — no game is actually being played today, so this grading is moot for today's slate regardless.
lessons_applied: F5-only signal without F1/F3 insufficient for GOLD tier
7/17 LAD@NYY DOWNGRADE lesson (same matchup, NYY lost 2-1)
live postponement check — game moved to 7/19

WSH @ OAK.reasoning: D-grade with severe dual opposing signals: J.T. Ginn (OAK) holds a 2.32 xERA advantage over Zack Littell (WSH), PQI delta=+34.8 opposes WSH (among the largest on the slate). Edge 16.08pp exceeds the 15pp ceiling. Market assigns WSH ~53.2% implied (fair_prob=0.468 for OAK) while model assigns 62.9% — a material calibration gap. Yesterday's WSH@OAK (different starters) was a 23-4 blowout despite identical opposition, flagged as 'direction correct, probability inflated' — that precedent is non-transferable to today's different pitcher pairing, so it supports the directional read (WSH) without justifying a stake. CONFIRM SKIP.

DET @ LAA.reasoning: Most extreme SKIP on the slate: Tarik Skubal (DET) holds a 3.78 xERA advantage over Grayson Rodriguez (LAA) — largest SP gap of any matchup today. PQI delta=-38.3 opposing LAA is the slate's largest opposition. fair_prob=0.336 (market gives LAA only 33.6%), below the 0.42 viability floor. Stage 1 gives LAA only 19.25% F5 probability, and Stage 1/2 disagree. This mirrors the same DET@LAA matchup from yesterday's slate (different starters), which also correctly stayed SKIP as the opposing signals predicted. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: Largest SP gap + largest PQI opposition on slate

PIT @ CLE (G2).reasoning: Probable SP for PIT (game 2) was not yet announced in the diag CSV. Live statsapi now shows Mason Montgomery (PIT) vs Logan Allen (CLE), with the game status 'Delayed Start' — still expected to play but too late for this pipeline pass to score. live SP confirmation for future scoring
